facebook.caption:
4.9 stars. 1,712 reviews. At a café on Trần Hưng Đạo.

Cafe Green(카페그린) in Dương Đông is not flying under the radar. The mango bingsu alone earns the trip. Shaved ice, fresh Phu Quoc mango, cold and heavy enough to split two ways.

If your café checklist doesn't have this on it yet, fix that.

Save this for your Phu Quoc trip.

41,284 reviews. 4.9 stars. Vinpearl Resort & Spa Phú Quốc is not a fluke.

Beachfront on Bãi Dài, one of Phu Quoc's longest stretches of sand. Golf course, full water park, multiple restaurants and bars. The kind of resort where you plan to leave and don't.

If you're doing Phu Quoc properly, this is the benchmark. Save this for your Phu Quoc trip.

facebook.caption:
749 reviews. 4.7 stars. And most tourists walk right past it.

Tween's Kitchen on Đường Trần Hưng Đạo is the real deal. Vietnamese food done right, priced right, in the middle of Phu Quoc's main drag. $15-30 a person and you'll leave full and happy.

No gimmicks. No tourist-trap menu. Just solid cooking that locals and repeat visitors keep coming back for.

Save this for your Phu Quoc trip.

facebook.caption:
This beach fixes everything.

Bãi Trường runs for miles along Đường Trần Hưng Đạo. Soft sand, shallow water, and the kind of quiet that actually slows your brain down. 4.3 stars across 718 reviews and it still feels underrated in person.

No crowds fighting for sunbeds. No hard sell. Just Phu Quoc doing what it does best.

Save this for your Phu Quoc trip.
